mappings.rationale: Section B covers the nature and scope of engineering including its professional character, education, and branches.
Section B.2 lists civil engineering as one of the major branches of engineering.
Structural engineering is part of the broader engineering disciplines covered in section B.2.
Section C covers the nature and scope of agricultural sciences including soil science, plant and animal production, and agricultural engineering.
Section D.1 covers bionics including mimicry of nature for engineering and the use of natural models for engineering problems, which connects to robotics.
Section D.1.b mentions the human brain as a model for computers and information-processing devices, directly connecting to AI.
Nanotechnology represents a recently developed interscience discipline (section D) combining physics, chemistry, and engineering.
Materials science is fundamental to engineering (section B) and connects to chemical and mechanical engineering branches.
Energy systems engineering connects to electrical engineering (B.2) and mechanical engineering as branches of the technological sciences.
Renewable energy technology is part of engineering (section B) involving electrical, chemical, and mechanical engineering disciplines.
Section B.2 lists nuclear engineering as a branch of engineering within the technological sciences.
The history of technological sciences (section A) includes military technology and its relationship to engineering development.
Innovation is central to the technological sciences and engineering, driving the development of new technologies and methods.
Section D.1 references computer memory devices and information-processing devices, connecting to the bionics-computing interface.
Section D.2 covers systems engineering and operations research, which involves network analysis and optimization.
Nuclear weapons technology relates to nuclear engineering (B.2) and the history of technological sciences (A).
Section D.2 covers systems engineering, which draws on complexity theory for analyzing and designing complex technical systems.
Computing technology is part of the history of technological sciences (section A) and connects to bionics and systems engineering (section D).
Landscape architecture involves engineering and agricultural science principles applied to land design and management.
